layman_headline: Extreme fear grips crypto — patience is the play, not panic buying.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is deep in 'Extreme Fear' territory at 22/100, with Bitcoin slipping below $62,500 as traders worry about a potential Fed rate hike and an upcoming inflation report. Altcoins are getting hit even harder, with most candidates down 10-30% in a week. While extreme fear historically creates buying opportunities, none of the current setups have strong enough technical confirmation — no clear volume breakouts, no MACD bullish crosses, and most coins are below all their moving averages with poor risk/reward ratios.
layman_action: Stay in cash and wait. The fear is real but the setups aren't clean yet. Watch for a stabilization in Bitcoin and oversold bounces with volume confirmation before committing capital.
expert_reasoning: In a market with Fear & Greed at 22 and BTC dominance at 56.1%, altcoins face a double headwind: macro fear and capital rotation to Bitcoin. While MON shows early signs of a reversal (bullish Marubozu, 5-day momentum turning up), it remains below all major moving averages with a neutral chart bias score of 0.0 and an unfavorable 0.79x risk/reward. GRASS had the sharpest 7-day drop (-32.6%) suggesting mean reversion potential, but its chart bias is slightly bearish (-0.45) and RSI at 47 isn't oversold enough. BONK is approaching oversold RSI (33) but has a strongly bearish chart (-1.40) with a bearish Marubozu — classic falling knife. The CoinDesk headline about traders lifting Fed rate hike bets is the key macro signal suppressing risk appetite. No volume breakout signals exist among any candidates, and our track record shows volume breakouts have a 100% win rate — waiting for that signal is the disciplined approach.
risk_factors: Inflation report could come in hot, triggering further risk-off selling across all crypto
BTC dominance at 56.1% means altcoins could continue bleeding even if BTC stabilizes
All candidates are below key moving averages with no confirmed MACD bullish crosses — catching falling knives
watch_list: Upcoming US inflation report — hot print could push Fear & Greed below 20 and create capitulation entry
BTC price action around $60,000 major support — a hold there could signal altcoin bottom
MON price action near $0.0185 support — a bounce with volume would be a quality entry signal
market_context: Crypto is in Extreme Fear (22/100) with BTC down to $62,442 as traders price in higher odds of a July Fed rate hike ahead of a key inflation report. Altcoins are underperforming with high BTC dominance at 56.1% — not the environment for aggressive alt entries.
context_banner: Extreme Fear creates opportunity, but discipline demands waiting for technical confirmation before deploying capital into a market facing macro headwinds.

key_signals.description: Extreme Fear at 22/100 with improving trend (up from yesterday's 28) — historically a contrarian buy zone but needs technical confirmation.
Bitcoin slipping as traders raise July Fed rate hike bets ahead of inflation report — macro uncertainty is the primary driver of risk-off sentiment.
MON RSI at 36.85 and BONK at 33.09 are approaching oversold, but neither has confirmed a reversal bounce with supporting volume.
Stablecoin adoption news (Bolivia USDT, Hyundai USDT settlement) is constructive for crypto long-term but not an immediate catalyst for altcoin recovery.
No volume breakout signals detected across any candidates — our highest-confidence signal (100% win rate) is absent, supporting a HOLD decision.
